content.description:
A reusable code module that exports functions, classes, or values for other code items to use.

## Purpose

Libraries provide shared functionality:
- **Utility functions** (string manipulation, data transformation)
- **Third-party code** (markdown-it, CodeMirror)
- **Domain logic** (tag tree building, search indexing)
- **UI components** (modal dialogs, form elements)

## Loading Libraries

Use `api.require()` with the library's name:

```javascript
// In a view or other code item
const modal = await api.require('modal-lib');
await modal.confirm('Delete this item?');

const md = await api.require('hobson-markdown');
const html = await md.render(markdownText, api);
```

## Creating a Library

```javascript
{
  id: crypto.randomUUID(),
  name: "my-utils",  // Name used with api.require()
  type: "66666666-0000-0000-0000-000000000000",  // kernel:library
  content: {
    description: "Utility functions for string manipulation",
    code: `
      export function capitalize(str) {
        return str.charAt(0).toUpperCase() + str.slice(1);
      }

      export function slugify(str) {
        return str.toLowerCase().replace(/\\s+/g, '-');
      }

      // Default export also works
      export default { capitalize, slugify };
    `
  }
}
```

## Third-Party Libraries

External JavaScript libraries can be stored as library items:

1. **UMD/Global libraries**: Store the minified source, library attaches to `window`
2. **ES modules**: Store the source directly, exports work naturally

Example: [markdown-it](item://08d5ecd2-01a1-43f1-ad50-314027db231a) stores the library source, then [markdown-it-wrapper](item://3a206d5a-54bb-4045-827e-20bf1a09f3de) wraps it with ES module exports.

## Common Libraries

| Library | Purpose |
|---------|--------|
| [modal-lib](item://2264e4e7-4ff7-4013-9f09-5393ff0e3116) | Modal dialogs, confirm, alert |
| [hobson-markdown](item://a1b2c3d4-5e6f-7a8b-9c0d-e1f2a3b4c5d6) | Markdown with item:// links and transclusion |
| [tag-tree-lib](item://98760f63-7e5f-40fb-b26f-a843f3f5b341) | Build hierarchical tag trees |
| [css-loader](item://c3cebc22-4a33-4182-b9a7-166316ba004e) | Load CSS items into document head |

## Libraries vs Views

| Aspect | Library | View |
|--------|---------|------|
| Purpose | Shared utilities | Render items |
| Main export | Any functions/values | `render(item, api)` |
| Loaded via | `api.require('name')` | Type chain dispatch |
| Has `for_type` | No | Yes |

## See Also

- [kernel:code](item://22222222-0000-0000-0000-000000000000) — Base type for all executable items
- [kernel:view](item://aaaaaaaa-0000-0000-0000-000000000000) — Code that renders items
- [kernel:module-system](item://33333333-4444-0000-0000-000000000000) — How modules are loaded and cached
